issues.title: Epic: Qartez indexing/runtime improvements
issues.description:
Root coordination epic for the Qartez indexing/runtime improvement program.

Tracks implementation work derived from .allium/qartez-indexing-improvements.allium and docs/agents/*.md.
issues.acceptance:
- Baseline acceptance/regression harness exists before major implementation work.
- Readiness contract prevents silent empty results during cold start or maintenance.
- Reader/writer DB split allows tool reads while indexing/watcher writes are active.
- Parser workers are lazy-loaded and avoid a global parser bottleneck.
- Full-index parse/extract can run in parallel without nondeterministic output drift.
- Hot-file incremental reparsing is faster on repeated edits and safely falls back to cold parse.
- Watcher and indexer use the same source-file eligibility policy.
- Large watcher/write batches are chunked and bounded.
- Shared DB lifecycle exposes stats, stale-root pruning, and explicit maintenance operations.
- Full validation passes before rollout: fmt, clippy, release build, release tests.
issues.context: Behavioural contract: .allium/qartez-indexing-improvements.allium. Workflow docs: docs/agents/beads-workflow.md, docs/agents/allium-contract.md, docs/agents/high-roi-implementation-playbook.md, docs/agents/validation-and-handoff.md.

issues.title: Baseline: acceptance harness for indexing/runtime behaviours
issues.description: Add regression coverage for the Allium indexing/runtime behaviour contract before major implementation lanes begin.
issues.acceptance:
- Cold-start readiness behaviour is tested.
- Deterministic indexing output is tested.
- Watcher/indexer eligibility parity is tested.
- Writer chunk boundedness is tested.
- Shared DB lifecycle metadata is testable.
- Hot-file reparse fallback is tested.
issues.context: This is the first executable task. It turns the Allium contract into guardrails before changing runtime architecture.

issues.title: Lane 1: Reader/writer DB split and readiness signalling
issues.description: Implement explicit readiness states and reader/writer DB separation for indexing, watcher writes, maintenance, and tool reads.
issues.acceptance:
- Cold-start query before index completion returns explicit deferred/not-ready status, not empty success.
- Tool reads can proceed while writer activity is active when a usable index exists.
- Indexing, watcher, prune, and compact use one controlled writer path.
- Maintenance state is visible to callers.
issues.context: Primary hotspots: startup/index orchestration, server DB wiring, watcher/index writer path, tool-handler read path.

issues.title: Lane 2: Thread-local lazy-loaded parser subsystem
issues.description: Replace the global parser bottleneck with lazy per-language parser workers suitable for parallel indexing.
issues.acceptance:
- Parser state is loaded lazily by language.
- Rust-only repos do not pay meaningful startup cost for absent languages.
- Parallel parse workers do not deadlock or serialize on one global parser lock.
- Parser metrics or tests prove per-language lazy activation.
issues.context: Primary hotspots: src/index/parser.rs and parser call sites in src/index/mod.rs.

issues.title: Lane 3: Parallel full-index parse/extract
issues.description: Parallelize full-index parse/extract while keeping DB writes deterministic and serialized or batched.
issues.acceptance:
- Full-index wall-clock time drops materially on representative repos.
- Result sets remain stable across repeated runs.
- DB writes are not performed directly from every worker.
- Output ordering remains deterministic where downstream behaviour depends on it.

issues.title: Lane 4: Hot-file incremental reparsing
issues.description: Reuse recent syntax tree state for watcher-driven edits and fall back safely to cold parse when reuse is unavailable.
issues.acceptance:
- Repeated edits on hot files reindex faster than cold parse baseline.
- Cache misses fall back to cold parse safely.
- Invalid or unavailable byte-edit information never causes stale-tree correctness bugs.
- Syntax trees are not persisted for the whole repo.
issues.context: Primary hotspots: parser/edit path and watcher-driven reindex path.

issues.title: Lane 5: Shared DB lifecycle and pruning
issues.description: Expose and manage shared database lifecycle state, stale roots, pruning, stats, and compaction.
issues.acceptance:
- Shared --db-path usage is visible to users.
- Root lifecycle metadata is tracked.
- Stale roots can be pruned safely only when idle.
- Stats surface DB size, root counts, and maintenance timestamps.
- Normal startup does not unexpectedly vacuum, compact, or prune.
issues.context: Primary hotspots: DB schema/open path, CLI/admin surfacing, project/root metadata.

issues.title: Lane 6: Watcher parity and chunked writes
issues.description: Make watcher eligibility match indexer eligibility and split heavy watcher writes into bounded chunks.
issues.acceptance:
- Watcher and indexer agree on supported files.
- Special filenames stay fresh.
- Write chunks remain at or below configured chunk size.
- Branch switches or large watcher bursts do not monopolize the DB for long stretches.
- Read latency remains bounded during large watcher bursts.
issues.context: Primary hotspots: src/watch.rs and supported-file logic shared with indexer.

issues.title: Bootstrap fresh shared-server database should not fail on empty Dolt commit
issues.description: Fix the fresh shared-server bootstrap path that fails when Dolt has nothing to commit.
issues.acceptance:
- Fresh shared-server bootstrap creates the project database.
- bd ready succeeds after bootstrap.
- Bootstrap treats an empty initial Dolt commit as success or avoids committing when there is no diff.
- Regression test covers the empty fresh DB bootstrap path.
issues.context: Observed on Windows PowerShell: bd bootstrap --dry-run planned create fresh database qartez_mcp_latest; bd bootstrap failed with 'dolt commit: Error 1105: nothing to commit'; bd init --force succeeded.

issues.title: Rollout: docs, regressions, and handoff protocol
issues.description: Update rollout documentation, regression coverage, and handoff guidance after implementation lanes land.
issues.acceptance:
- docs/agents guidance reflects implemented behaviour.
- Allium is updated for any observable behaviour changes.
- Full validation passes: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo build --release; cargo test --release --no-fail-fast.
- Installer/bootstrap changes run the relevant install tests.
issues.context: Use docs/agents/validation-and-handoff.md as the closeout checklist.
